test_cases.title: Low Battery Detection
steps: Set the device to simulate 300 measurement cycles
Measure blood pressure 3 times to simulate typical use
Verify the low battery icon is displayed
test_cases.expected_result: The low battery icon should be displayed

test_cases.title: Overpressure Protection - Single Exceedance
steps: Set the cuﬀ pressure to 300 mmHg
Trigger the emergency deﬂation valve to simulate sensor fault
Verify the device halts inﬂation and vents the cuﬀ within 2 seconds
test_cases.expected_result: The device should trigger the emergency deﬂation valve and vent the cuﬀ within 2 seconds

test_cases.title: Overpressure Protection - Prolonged Exceedance
steps: Set the cuﬀ pressure to 300 mmHg and hold for 3 seconds
Trigger the emergency deﬂation valve to simulate sensor fault
Verify the device halts inﬂation and vents the cuﬀ within 2 seconds
test_cases.expected_result: The device should trigger the emergency deﬂation valve and vent the cuﬀ within 2 seconds

test_cases.title: Low Battery Icon Display
steps: Set the device to measure blood pressure three times a day.
Wait until the device displays a low battery icon.
Verify the icon is displayed correctly.
test_cases.expected_result: The low battery icon is displayed once the remaining capacity falls below 15%.

test_cases.title: Battery Life Under Typical Use
steps: Set the device to measure blood pressure three times a day.
Monitor the device's battery life until it reaches 15% capacity.
Verify the device has approximately 300 measurement cycles before requiring battery replacement.
test_cases.expected_result: The device has approximately 300 measurement cycles before requiring battery replacement.

test_cases.title: Battery Life Calculation
steps: Set the device to measure blood pressure three times a day.
Calculate the number of measurement cycles after 15% battery capacity is reached.
Verify the calculated value is close to 300 measurement cycles.
test_cases.expected_result: The calculated value is close to 300 measurement cycles.
